categories.description: Assessment of Sort practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: The area is clear of excess equipment, paperwork, or other types of materials.
The area looks organized.
The area is clear of excess personal items or is appropriately placed.
The area is clear of outdated manuals, brochures, visual performance charts, etc.
Electronic files are organized and only recent ones are available.
Emails are not kept longer than a certain period of time to ensure time not wasted looking for one.

categories.name: Set In Order

attachments.name: labeling_system_example.jpg
attachments.description: Example of effective labeling system implementation

categories.description: Assessment of Set In Order practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: A place for everything and everything in its place.
Items are labelled.
Standard procedures and information are visible and available.
There are no items on the floor that interrupt workflow.
Items are returned to their proper location after use.
Standard supplies and stock are appropriate to demand and available.

categories.name: Shine
categories.description: Assessment of Shine practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: Equipment is regularly cleaned and protected to maintain high performance.
The standard cleaning schedule is displayed.
The standard equipment maintenance schedules are displayed.
The area looks clean, bright, and inviting.
Equipment and facilities are in excellent condition.
Trash and recycling bins are empty.

categories.name: Standardize
categories.description: Assessment of Standardize practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: 5S evaluation forms are posted, utilized, and current.
An improvement idea list is posted.
There is consistency in the workplace.
Visual guidance exists for workplace standards.
Job or cross-training is ongoing.
Standards for the area exist and are available.

categories.name: Sustain
categories.description: Assessment of Sustain practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: The standards are actually followed.
Time is dedicated daily to sustain 5S.
All equipment and supplies have an identified owner.
5S is regularly audited with posted results.
Recognition is given to teams with high 5S scores.
5S is part of daily culture.

categories.name: Safety
categories.description: Assessment of Safety practices in Main Production Floor

statements.name: Safety equipment is in place and accessible.
Emergency contact information is posted.
Personal protective equipment is available, utilized and in good condition.
Fire extinguishers are available and serviced.
Safety records are posted.
Emergency exit and routes are clear.

keyFindings: Safety Excellence: Outstanding safety culture with near-perfect scores (4.7/5.0 average)
Strong Physical Organization: Good performance in Set In Order and Shine practices (4.2/5.0 average)
Digital Gap: Electronic file and email management significantly lag behind physical organization (2.0/5.0)
Sustainability Challenge: Weak daily routines and audit processes hinder sustained improvements (2.5/5.0)
Visual Standards Need: Limited visual management and standardization tools in place (2.8/5.0)

strengthAreas.highlights: Exemplary safety equipment availability, clear emergency protocols, strong PPE compliance
strengthAreas.bestPractices: Accessible safety equipment, posted emergency contacts, clear exit routes, regular fire extinguisher servicing

strengthAreas.highlights: Excellent labeling system, clear item placement, unobstructed workflow areas
strengthAreas.bestPractices: Everything has a designated place, effective labeling, standard procedures visible, no floor obstructions

strengthAreas.highlights: Clean and inviting workspace, well-maintained equipment, effective waste management
strengthAreas.bestPractices: Regular equipment cleaning, bright work areas, empty trash/recycling bins, excellent facility condition

improvementOpportunities.gaps: Digital clutter in electronic files and emails, some physical organization issues remain
improvementOpportunities.recommendations: Implement digital file retention policies, establish email archival system, create red-tag process for physical items

improvementOpportunities.gaps: No dedicated 5S time, irregular audits, lack of recognition system
improvementOpportunities.recommendations: Schedule daily 5S time blocks, implement weekly audits with posted results, create recognition program for high performers

improvementOpportunities.gaps: Missing visual standards, outdated evaluation forms, limited cross-training
improvementOpportunities.recommendations: Install visual management boards, update 5S evaluation forms, develop cross-training matrix

learningPoints: Safety First Foundation: This production floor demonstrates how prioritizing safety creates a strong foundation for all other 5S practices. When workers feel safe, they're more engaged in improvement efforts.

Physical vs Digital Balance: The stark contrast between well-organized physical spaces (4.2/5.0) and poorly managed digital assets (2.0/5.0) highlights a common blind spot in modern 5S implementation.
Sustaining Success: Even with strong initial implementation in Sort, Set In Order, and Shine, without robust sustaining mechanisms, improvements gradually erode over time.
Visual Management Power: The correlation between areas with visual standards and higher performance scores demonstrates the importance of making standards visible and obvious.
Culture Building Journey: The gap between having standards (4/5) and following them (3/5) illustrates that 5S is ultimately about culture change, not just procedures.
nextSteps: Launch Digital 5S Initiative focusing on file organization and email management systems
Implement Daily 5S Time with 15-minute blocks per shift for sustaining activities
Install Visual Management Center with audit results, improvement ideas, and recognition board
Develop 5S Champion Network with representatives from each shift to drive culture change
Create Monthly 5S Recognition Program tied to audit scores and improvement suggestions

organizationalMaturity.readinessForAdvancement: Ready for standardization and sustainability focus to advance to next level

benchmarkComparison.positioning: Above industry average overall, but sustainability practices lag behind top performers
benchmarkComparison.competitiveInsights: Safety scores match top performers, indicating strong foundation for building comprehensive 5S culture

actionItems.title: Implement Daily 5S Routine Schedule
actionItems.description: Establish a structured daily 5S maintenance routine with 15-minute time blocks allocated for each shift to sustain workplace organization

attachments.name: 5s_schedule_template.jpg
attachments.description: Daily 5S maintenance schedule template for production floor

actionItems.title: Digital File Organization System
actionItems.description: Create standardized folder structures and file naming conventions for electronic documents, with automatic archival of files older than 6 months

attachments.name: file_organization_after.jpg
attachments.description: After: Clean, organized digital filing system

actionItems.title: Visual Management Board Setup
actionItems.description: Install visual management boards displaying 5S audit results, improvement ideas, and recognition for high-performing areas

actionItems.title: Email Retention Policy Implementation
actionItems.description: Implement automated email retention policy with 90-day retention for general emails and 1-year for important business communications
